sutta: the buddha said monks there are these 8 years in merit and goodness the food of happiness celestial resulting in happiness leading heavenward which are conducive to what is pleasing lovely precious beneficial and to happiness 1 aid here in monks an aryan disciple has found refuge in the buddha this monks is the first yield in merit and goodness the food of happiness etc again monks an aryan disciple has found refuge in dhamma this is the second eu in merit and goodness again monks an aryan disciple has found refuge in the sangha this is the third yield in merit and goodness the food of happiness celestial resulting in happiness leading heavenward which conduces to what is pleasing lovely precious beneficial and to happiness monks there are these 5 gifts great gifts recognized from the first known for many a day known by tradition ancient and unconfounded not being confounded in the past they have not become confounded nor will they become confounded they are not scorned by discerning recluse or brahmins what 5 herein monks an aryan disciple abandons taking life and abstains there from thus abstaining to unnumbered beings he gives fearlessness he gives non-hatred he gives non-ill will and in giving fearlessness non-hatred non-ill will he becomes a partaker in unbounded fearlessness enmity and goodwill this month is the first gift and scorned by discerning recluse and brahmanas this monks is the fourth yield in merit and goodness the food of happiness etc again monks and aryan disciples abandon stealing and abandons all lustful evil ways abandons lying abandons the use of intoxicants which cause indolence and abstains therefrom this monks is the fifth gift a gift a great gift recognized from the first known for many a day known by tradition ancient and unconfounded not being confounded in the past it has not become confounded nor will it become confounded it is not scorned by discerning recluse of brahmin this monks is the eighth yield in merit and goodness the food of happiness celestial resulting in happiness leading heavenward which conduces to what is pleasing lovely precious beneficial and to happiness amongst these are the heals in merit and goodness as the end of the sutta
commentary: so here the buddha is saying there are 8 things which are meritorious and results in uh leading a person to the heavens and the first 3 are the 3 refugees taking refuge in the buddha dharma and sangha and that is basically means a person understands the dhamma [Music] and after that there are the 5 precepts so if a person has these 8 things and the 3 refuges and the 5 precepts that person will have a lot of merit and a lot of blessings that leads have been words released to what is pleasing lovely precious beneficial and to happiness the next is
